title: task
description: python/src/lichen/routing/router.py:596 - The _haversine function computes a = sin²(Δlat/2) + cos(lat1)·cos(lat2)·sin²(Δlon/2), which theoretically maxes at 1.0 for antipodal points. Due to floating point errors, a can be slightly > 1 (e.g., 1.0000000000001), causing math.sqrt(a) > 1 and math.asin() to raise ValueError. Fix: clamp before sqrt with math.sqrt(min(1.0, a)).
